title: baccharis linearifolia subsp. polycephala
intro: Baccharis linearifolia subsp. polycephala is a subspecies of shrub in the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the "Til" or "Coyote Brush", native to the Americas. The medicinal properties of Baccharis linearifolia subsp. polycephala lie in its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Baccharis linearifolia subsp. polycephala can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
